# UC-10.16.11 · Microsoft 365 Defender Open Incident Backlog and Severity Mix

## Description

Counts non-resolved Microsoft 365 Defender incidents by severity so leadership can see backlog growth, analyst capacity risk, and whether critical incidents are stuck open beyond SLA.

## Value

Gives a single-pane backlog metric tied to native Defender incident workflow, complementing alert-level searches and helping prioritize triage hiring or playbook automation.

## Implementation

Enable incident collection in the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security. Normalize `status` vocabulary (`active` vs `inProgress`) with eval if your tenant returns mixed casing. Exclude archived or historical replays if your input duplicates incident updates. Alert when high-severity open incidents exceed a threshold or when total open incidents grows sharply day over day.

## SPL

```spl
index=* sourcetype="ms365:defender:incident" earliest=-7d
| eval st=lower(coalesce(status, Status, ""))
| where st IN ("active","new","in_progress","investigating") OR (st!="resolved" AND st!="closed")
| eval sev=lower(coalesce(severity, Severity, "unknown"))
| stats dc(incidentId) as open_incidents by sev
| sort -open_incidents
```

## Visualization

Column chart (open incidents by severity), single value (total open), timechart of open incident count.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6207)
